summary: Sprint T2 backend regression — 26/26 pytest tests PASSED against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. All /api/trading/* endpoints (runtime/status, verdict BTC/ETH/SOL, opportunities, paper account/positions/orders/events, submit LONG override with sizeUsd=1500, duplicate-guard, close with pnlUsd/pnlPct, evaluate-hits, account stats update) still behave identically to Sprint T1. Cognition surfaces (/api/ta/basic/BTC, /api/sentiment/runtime/BTC, /api/fractal/runtime/BTC), /api/miniapp/lite and /api/panel/admin still return 200. Legacy /api/terminal/* and /api/terminal-app/* still honest-404. Expo web shell at GET / serves 200 with the expected bundle markers.
critical_code_review_comments: No backend Python files were modified in Sprint T2 — frontend-only wiring. Regression confirms zero behavioral drift on /api/trading/* and cognition surfaces.

Submit endpoint (POST /api/trading/paper/submit) with override LONG and sizeUsd=1500 correctly returns ok:true with orderId, positionId, entry, stop, target (stop < entry < target invariant verified).
Close endpoint (POST /api/trading/paper/close) returns ok:true with pnlUsd, pnlPct, and reason='manual'.
OPEN positions listing includes live unrealizedPnlUsd, unrealizedPnlPct, currentPrice (frontend Exposure screen can render these directly).
After close, position is reflected in CLOSED listing with closePrice and closeReason populated; events ledger contains ORDER_FILLED and POSITION_CLOSED entries.
Verdict payload includes blockedBy[] reasons (frontend can surface these on the Deployment screen when action=WAIT).
Backend logs are clean — only benign FastAPI duplicate-operation-id warnings for unrelated telegram stub (pre-existing, not introduced by T2).

success_rate.frontend: N/A (skipped per request — T2 UI is gated behind app-mode + operator capability flow)

seed_data_creation: Uses the dedicated test account 'TEST_t1_paper_acct' (auto-created by _ensure_account). A single LONG BTC position is opened and then closed during the flow; pre-flow cleanup helper closes any leftover OPEN positions on that account so the suite is idempotent. Production 'default-paper-account' is untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Trading Runtime backend is stable across T1 (iteration_2) and T2 (iteration_3). The regression file /app/backend/tests/test_trading_runtime_t1.py now also pings the Expo web root (GET /) to catch frontend bundle regressions. Re-run with: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=<public-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_trading_runtime_t1.py -v. When the main agent requests a frontend pass on TRADE/PORTFOLIO screens, the testIDs and selectors will need to be added to TradeScreen.tsx and PortfolioScreen.tsx first — current screens were skipped per the agent_to_agent_context_note for this iteration.
